concise_description: Created a fail-closed symlink installer, global working agreements, command rules, and six reusable skills while explicitly excluding live client state and credentials.

milestone_name: One harness for Codex and Claude
concise_description: Reorganized client-specific guidance around a shared skill directory and added Claude discovery, establishing a dual-client rather than Codex-only harness.
Added dependency-free inventory, profile comparison, doctor checks, captured fixtures, and the first phase-one suite for seven logical environments.
Converted reviewed plans into bounded apply transactions with collision refusal, manifests, partial-failure rollback, and explicit rollback commands.
Added append-only shell transactions that preserve prior bytes and roll back only unchanged managed suffixes.
Added one-tool transactions with checksummed archives, version checks, stable links, and rollback integrity checks.

Recorded an agent-launched rm -rf against the real account home after a temporary-HOME assignment expired; containment, partial restoration, unrecoverable state, and paused fleet work were made explicit.
evidence_paths: historical TODO.md at e5200fd;historical TODO.md at d726f0d;presentation/evidence/incident-rm-rf.md

concise_description: Introduced a manifest-and-token workflow that rejects protected roots and revalidates exact recursive-deletion targets before and after removal.
Added the seven-node Restic topology and manual backup, full-data check, restore, and independent encrypted-generation gates.
Added exactly-one-successor native scheduler chains without login cron, retention deletion, or central polling.
Added a frozen synthetic corpus, paired baseline/candidate runner, bounded retries, private raw evidence, and aggregate reporting.

milestone_name: Evaluation evidence could reject a plausible change
concise_description: Published the 70-run T-181 aggregate: 69 deterministic passes, zero safety failures, and no evidence-based benefit for the candidate after targeted review.

milestone_name: Portable CI became a merge gate
concise_description: Added read-only hosted CI for the portable suite, later used as the protected main-branch required check.
Recorded passing compiler, Python, CTest, and bounded sanitizer evidence across all seven managed nodes using visible native routes.
Recorded driver/runtime readiness on seven nodes and CUDA-kernel passes on five, with two explicit toolkit skips rather than inferred failures.
Added prerequisite-bound Git-bundle synchronization that refuses dirty or divergent checkouts and preserves partially completed fleet runs.
Deleted login-time fetch/fast-forward and exit-time publish behavior after it proved too implicit; Git synchronization and publication became explicit operations.
Removed website-specific tools, audits, and ruleset ownership and added a regression test preventing dependency on a sibling repository.

milestone_name: Public engine plus private Mac intent
concise_description: Added a Darwin-specific, value-minimized plan/doctor/control engine with a strict owner-controlled private companion rather than putting Mac identity or payloads in public Git.

milestone_name: Cross-platform Bash and tmux replaced private duplication
concise_description: Moved Bash hooks and tmux configuration into the public cross-platform control plane and added a recoverable bridge from the historical private bundle to SSH-only private intent.
Added explicit public Codex and Claude settings transactions, local preservation state, rollback drills, and fleet catch-up surfaces.
Reversed an earlier launcher-ownership design: the harness manages policy and a separate wrapper while the native client remains locally owned.
Added verify-before-promotion replacement, predecessor restoration after interruption, unchanged-only rollback, and explicit rejection of unsafe downgrade or partial layouts.
Added a client-neutral driver/co-pilot protocol with staged exchange, immutable evidence, reciprocal critique, external seals, receipts, and driver-only target execution.
Bound prompts into receipts, added read-only status and bounded waiting, made test workers affinity-aware, and removed duplicated CI steps without removing the complete gate.

milestone_name: HEAD is a transactional multi-platform control plane
concise_description: HEAD combines shared policy, profiles, 43 user-facing commands including guarded deletion, 57 focused suites, Linux/HPC fleet operations, personal-Mac onboarding, backup, evaluation, and symmetric cowork.
